reasoning: Hallucinations are largely considered an inherent feature of current autoregressive LLM architectures (like Transformers), arising from their probabilistic nature of predicting the next token. While techniques like RAG, better RLHF, and chain-of-thought can reduce hallucination rates, eliminating them entirely by the end of 2025 is widely recognized as impossible without a fundamental, unprecedented paradigm shift in AI architectures. The resolution criteria specifically states that if the problem 'still exists', it will resolve NO. Given that hallucinations will likely still occur to some extent under edge cases or missing knowledge, the problem will not be considered completely 'solved'. The market freeze value at ~7.8% additionally supports a very low probability of this resolving YES.
